technical_documents.description: An off-grid, zero-energy bioclimatic cultivation infrastructure designed for highly sensitive macrofungi. Utilizing a double-walled bamboo framework insulated with an active bio-strata matrix, this chamber harnesses natural convection loops and micro-capillary evaporation to stabilize relative humidity and inner temperature without mechanical HVAC dependencies.
technical_documents.problem_statement: Commercial mushroom cultivation demands 24/7 HVAC loads to tightly regulate an 85–95% atmospheric humidity threshold, burning through over 40% of standard farm operational expenditures. This massive utility overhead effectively excludes localized off-grid smallholders from capturing equity within the $50B global mushroom industry.
technical_documents.engineering_solution: The architecture incorporates a dual-skin structural bamboo envelope framing a 5cm internal cavity packed with a thermodynamic 50:50 mix of porous activated charcoal and high-absorbency coconut coir. When hydrated, this capillary medium serves as a passive evaporative cooling wall. Working in tandem with a dense thatched thermal roof and strict micro-climate alignment (north-facing orientation), the chamber triggers internal natural convection currents. This fluid movement drops latent ambient heat to maintain a constant inner range of 22–26°C and a stable relative humidity of 88% completely powered by kinetic vapor pressure differential.

bill_of_materials.name: Structural Structural Bamboo Poles (3m Length, x20)
Inoculated High-Yield Oyster Mushroom Spawn Culture (5 kg)

specifications.humidity_control: Passive evaporative cooling via dual-layer bio-strata wall integration

step_by_step_build: Step 1: Build a 2m x 2m x 2.5m bamboo frame with double-wall spacing of 5cm between inner and outer poles.
Step 2: Pack the wall cavity tightly with a 50:50 mix of soaked coconut coir and crushed charcoal.
Step 3: Pasteurise rice straw in 65°C water for 1 hour; drain and cool before inoculating with spawn.
Step 4: Hang inoculated straw logs from horizontal bamboo crossbars at 30cm spacing.
Step 5: Mist the coir-charcoal wall fill daily; harvest first flush at 14–18 days.

comments.text: Getting 3 flushes per log. Zero electricity. The coir-charcoal wall keeps humidity at 88% consistently.
